title: Crunchy Spicy Polish Beef Stew
description: A spicy and light one-pot Polish dish featuring mixed beef cuts and lamination-puff, suitable for a sugar-free diet.
instructions: Cut the beef brisket and shank into 1-inch cubes and season with salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Heat the lard or oil in a large pot over medium-high heat and brown the beef cubes in batches.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the caraway seeds to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Sprinkle the flour over the beef and onion mixture and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the beef broth to the pot, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Transfer the pot to the oven and bake for 20 minutes to crisp the top.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
